title: viola purpurea var. charlestonensis
intro: Viola purpurea var. charlestonensis, also known as the Charleston violet or Woodland pansy, is a plant species in the violet family that is native to the southeastern United States. The medicinal properties of Viola purpurea var. charlestonensis include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits associated with this plant include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce pain and inflammation, and support immune function. The herbal preparations of Viola purpurea var. charlestonensis typically involve making a tea from its leaves and flowers, or using it in tincture or salve form to harness its medicinal properties.
